summary: Legendary Colgate University show with killer playing and crisp sound‑board recording, though a few brief cuts in the drum and Iko Iko tracks.
review: Fans rave about this performance – the first set is described as an A+ burst of tight jams from “Bertha” through “Let It Grow,” and the second set delivers standout moments like the fast‑paced “Eyes of the World > Estimated Prophet” and a superb “Johnny B. Goode” encore. The updated SBD (soundboard) source sounds clean and modern, but listeners note short drop‑outs in the drum break and the Iko Iko segment.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are highly enthusiastic about both the musical performance and the sound‑board quality; minor recording cuts keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered SBD sounds clear and modern, praised as “crisp” and “nice sounding,” but contains short edits (58 s in Drums, 23 s in Iko Iko) that some listeners note.

standout_songs: Eyes of the World > Estimated Prophet
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set flows tightly with high‑energy jams and strong transitions, earning an A+ grade; the second set is solid but slightly uneven (B‑), highlighted by a fast “Eyes of the World” sequence and ending on a fiery “Johnny B. Goode.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Stella is noted as the roughest part for Jerry, but overall his guitar work is praised throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s long funny during tech difficulties and his bass action around the six‑minute mark of “Eyes of the World” are highlighted; he adds capital on “Let It Grow.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith shines on the “Estimated Prophet” transition and delivers a great solo on the “Johnny B. Goode” encore.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum break has a 58‑second edit, but Bill’s playing is otherwise solid; Phil’s action around 6 min in the second set is noted.
